title: CI/CD Vault Configuration Drift Ecosystem Audit
scope: All Receptor repositories with deploy workflows, receptor-infra

findings.description: Five JWT roles referenced in production deploy workflows (ci-preference-frontend, ci-planner-frontend, ci-website-frontend, ci-workforce-frontend, ci-match-backend) have no documented creation procedure, no bootstrap script, and no policy definition in receptor-infra. ci-website-frontend confirmed missing from live Vault.

tasks.description: Create vault/roles/ directory in receptor-infra with one YAML file per JWT role documenting role_name, bound_audiences, bound_claims, policies, and TTL.
SSH to cluster and create all 5 missing JWT roles in live Vault using the role definitions from VD-01-T1.
tasks.targetFile: MANUAL — SSH to cluster, requires root token

tasks.description: Update docs/security/vault-configuration.md to document the 5 new roles alongside existing github-actions-role and receptor-infra-tf-ci.

findings.description: The secret path secret/infrastructure/github-app-deploy-bot (used by all 5 deploy workflows) does not appear in any bootstrap script, documentation, or manifest in receptor-infra. It exists only on the live cluster (if at all). The documented GitHub App path is secret/ci/github-app.

tasks.description: SSH to cluster and verify whether secret/infrastructure/github-app-deploy-bot exists in Vault KV. Document the result.
tasks.targetFile: MANUAL — SSH to cluster, requires root token

tasks.description: Create vault/secrets/README.md documenting every KV path, its fields, which roles can read it, and the bootstrap command. No actual secret values.
Create or update a Vault policy (ci-deploy-bot) that explicitly grants read access to the github-app-deploy-bot path. Write to vault/policies/ci-deploy-bot.hcl.

findings.description: No declarative Vault role management exists. Vault roles/policies are created via ad-hoc SSH commands not captured in version control. No vault/roles/ directory, no HCL policy files, no idempotent bootstrap script for JWT roles.

tasks.description: Create vault/bootstrap-jwt-roles.sh — an idempotent script that reads vault/roles/*.yaml and vault/policies/*.hcl and applies them to Vault. Must accept a VAULT_TOKEN parameter.

findings.description: vault-action secrets path is inconsistent: preferencer-frontend uses logical path with kv-version: 2, all others use explicit /data/ path without kv-version. Commit history shows 6 rounds of path changes alternating between formats.

tasks.description: Standardise all 5 deploy workflows to use the logical path format (secret/infrastructure/...) with explicit kv-version: 2. This is the correct pattern per hashicorp/vault-action@v3 documentation.
tasks.targetFile: ALL — deploy.yml in preferencer-frontend, planner-frontend, website-frontend, workforce-frontend, match-backend

findings.description: preferencer-frontend fetches both private_key and app_id from Vault. The other 4 repos read DEPLOY_BOT_APP_ID from GitHub Secrets, creating potential split-brain if Vault is updated independently.

tasks.description: Update all 4 remaining deploy workflows to fetch app_id from Vault instead of GitHub Secrets, matching the preferencer-frontend pattern.
tasks.targetFile: ALL — deploy.yml in planner-frontend, website-frontend, workforce-frontend, match-backend

findings.description: The deploy workflow YAML is copy-pasted across 5 repositories with no shared reusable workflow. Each copy drifts independently, as proven by VD-04.

tasks.description: Create a reusable workflow in receptor-infra (.github/workflows/deploy-gitops.yml) parameterised by app-name, image-repo, and manifest-path. All 5 repos call this via workflow_call.
Refactor all 5 deploy.yml files to call the reusable workflow instead of inlining the Vault + token + manifest update steps.
tasks.targetFile: ALL — deploy.yml in all 5 repos (refactor to call reusable workflow)

findings.description: supabase status -o env output parsed with grep '^ANON_KEY=' fails when output contains ANSI codes, leading whitespace, or stderr interleaving. planner-frontend CI run 23477490024 shows 120s timeout despite Supabase being fully started.

tasks.description: Create a shared supabase-start composite action (or reusable script) that handles DinD readiness, supabase start, status polling with robust key extraction (strip ANSI, handle stderr), and GITHUB_ENV export. Use in all frontend CI workflows.
tasks.targetFile: ALL — ci-resilience.yml in planner-frontend, preference-frontend, workforce-frontend

findings.description: The ecosystem has no agent-readable infrastructure contracts. Vault roles, policies, and secret paths exist only on the live cluster (SSH + root token required). Agents cannot validate workflow configurations against a source of truth. Result: ~40 vault-related fix commits in 6 days, most incorrect.

tasks.description: Implement the 'Crawl' tier of agentic platform engineering: codify all Vault roles, policies, and secret contracts as declarative files in receptor-infra. Tasks VD-01-T1, VD-02-T2, VD-03-T1 are prerequisites.
tasks.targetFile: DEPENDS — on VD-01-T1, VD-02-T2, VD-03-T1 completion

tasks.description: Create .agents/infrastructure-contracts.md in receptor-infra — a single document that agents can read to understand the mapping between workflows (role, path, audience) and Vault configuration.

findings.description: No pre-flight check validates a workflow's vault-action role, secrets path, and audience against documented Vault configuration before runtime. Errors only discovered after commit to main.

tasks.description: Add a CI job in receptor-infra that scans org-wide deploy.yml files and validates vault-action parameters against vault/roles/ and vault/policies/ definitions. Depends on VD-01-T1 and VD-02-T2.
tasks.targetFile: DEPENDS — on VD-01-T1 and VD-02-T2 completion

findings.description: The 260319-cicd-workflow-health audit (Closed) produced findings DR-24/DR-15/DR-28 requiring Vault OIDC for deploy. Implementation created current failures because prerequisite Vault infrastructure was never provisioned. The audit DoD lacked a Vault bootstrap verification gate.

tasks.description: Add a 'prerequisite infrastructure' section to the audit-document-standards skill: any finding that requires infrastructure provisioning (Vault roles, k8s CRDs, DNS records) must include a verification command in the task definition.

clarifications.decision: kubectl exec into vault-0 pod is the only way to run vault commands. Listing JWT roles requires a root or privileged token (403 without auth). Live verification requires generating a root token via 3-of-5 recovery key shares.

The correct pattern for hashicorp/vault-action@v3 with KV-v2 is logical path (secret/infrastructure/...) with kv-version: 2. The action internally appends /data/. Using secret/data/... without kv-version may produce double /data/data/ depending on auto-detection.

The Microsoft GBB 'Agentic Platform Engineering' framework (cluster-doctor pattern, crawl-walk-run) is the reference architecture for the proposed overhaul. See https://github.com/microsoftgbb/agentic-platform-engineering

implementationPhases.name: Unblock Deployments — Vault Provisioning
implementationPhases.rationale: VD-01 and VD-02 are the root cause of all current deploy failures. Without the Vault roles and secret path, no deploy workflow can run.
implementationPhases.description: Create and apply the missing Vault JWT roles and verify the github-app-deploy-bot secret path. This unblocks all 5 deploy workflows immediately.

implementationPhases.name: Standardise Workflow Configuration
implementationPhases.rationale: Once deployments work, standardise the configuration that caused the drift to prevent recurrence.
implementationPhases.description: Unify vault-action path format, app_id source, and supabase readiness detection across all repos.

implementationPhases.name: Infrastructure as Code for Vault
implementationPhases.rationale: Prevents future drift by making Vault configuration version-controlled and agent-readable.
implementationPhases.description: Create declarative Vault role/policy definitions in receptor-infra with an idempotent bootstrap script and agent-readable contracts.

implementationPhases.name: Eliminate Drift — Shared Workflows
implementationPhases.rationale: Structural prevention: shared workflows eliminate copy-paste drift, CI contract validation catches errors before runtime, and hardened DoD prevents future audit regressions.
implementationPhases.description: Create reusable deploy workflow, add CI contract validation, and harden audit DoD with infrastructure verification gates.
